toolUseResult.originalFile:
import { oc } from "@orpc/contract";
import { z } from "zod";
import {
	DaysInputSchema,
	DeveloperCostBreakdownSchema,
	DeveloperDetailsInputSchema,
	DeveloperDetailsSchema,
	DeveloperErrorSchema,
	DeveloperFeatureUsageSchema,
	DeveloperProjectSchema,
	DeveloperSessionSchema,
	DeveloperSessionsInputSchema,
	DeveloperSummarySchema,
	DeveloperTimelineSchema,
	DeveloperTrendDataPointSchema,
	DimensionAnalysisDataPointSchema,
	DimensionAnalysisInputSchema,
	ErrorTrendDataPointSchema,
	ErrorTrendsInputSchema,
	InsightSchema,
	LearningEntrySchema,
	LearningsFeedStatsSchema,
	LearningsTrendDataPointSchema,
	LearningsTrendInputSchema,
	ModelTokensTrendDataSchema,
	OverviewKPIsSchema,
	PaginatedDaysInputSchema,
	ProjectContributorSchema,
	ProjectCostBreakdownSchema,
	ProjectDetailDataSchema,
	ProjectDetailsInputSchema,
	ProjectErrorSchema,
	ProjectFeatureUsageSchema,
	ProjectInvestmentSchema,
	ProjectTrendDataPointSchema,
	RecurringErrorSchema,
	RecurringErrorsInputSchema,
	ROIMetricsSchema,
	ROITrendSchema,
	SessionAnalyticsSchema,
	SessionAnalyticsSummaryComparisonSchema,
	SessionAnalyticsSummarySchema,
	SessionDetailInputSchema,
	SessionDetailSchema,
	SessionListInputSchema,
	SuccessRateSchema,
	TeamSummaryComparisonSchema,
	UsageTrendDataSchema,
} from "./schemas/analytics.js";

export * from "./schemas/analytics.js";

export const HealthSchema = z.object({
	status: z.literal("ok"),
	timestamp: z.number(),
});

export const UserSchema = z.object({
	id: z.string(),
	email: z.string(),
	name: z.string(),
	image: z.string().nullable(),
	activeOrganizationId: z.string().nullable(),
});

export const OrganizationSchema = z.object({
	id: z.string(),
	name: z.string(),
	slug: z.string(),
	logo: z.string().nullable(),
});

export const SessionTagSchema = z.enum([
	"research",
	"new_feature",
	"bug_fix",
	"refactoring",
	"documentation",
	"tests",
	"other",
]);

export type { Source } from "./schemas/source.js";
export { SourceSchema } from "./schemas/source.js";

import { SourceSchema } from "./schemas/source.js";

export const SubagentFileSchema = z.object({
	agentId: z.string(),
	content: z.string(),
});

export const IngestSessionInputSchema = z.object({
	source: SourceSchema.default("claude_code"),
	sessionId: z.string(),
	projectPath: z.string(),
	repository: z.string().optional(),
	gitRemote: z.string().optional(),
	packageName: z.string().optional(),
	gitBranch: z.string().optional(),
	gitSha: z.string().optional(),
	tag: SessionTagSchema.optional(),
	content: z.string(),
	subagents: z.array(SubagentFileSchema).optional(),
	organizationId: z.string().optional(),
});

export const IngestSessionOutputSchema = z.object({
	success: z.literal(true),
	sessionId: z.string(),
});

export type IngestSessionInput = z.infer<typeof IngestSessionInputSchema>;

export const contract = {
	health: oc.output(HealthSchema),
	me: oc.output(UserSchema),
	listMyOrganizations: oc.output(z.array(OrganizationSchema)),
	ingestSession: oc
		.input(IngestSessionInputSchema)
		.output(IngestSessionOutputSchema),
	getOrganizationSessionCount: oc
		.input(z.object({ organizationId: z.string() }))
		.output(z.object({ count: z.number() })),
	deleteOrganization: oc
		.input(
			z.object({
				organizationId: z.string(),
				migrateSessionsTo: z.string().optional(),
			}),
		)
		.output(z.object({ success: z.literal(true) })),
	analytics: {
		overview: {
			kpis: oc.input(DaysInputSchema).output(OverviewKPIsSchema),
			usageTrend: oc
				.input(DaysInputSchema)
				.output(z.array(UsageTrendDataSchema)),
			modelTokensTrend: oc
				.input(DaysInputSchema)
				.output(z.array(ModelTokensTrendDataSchema)),
			insights: oc.input(DaysInputSchema).output(z.array(InsightSchema)),
			teamSummaryComparison: oc
				.input(DaysInputSchema)
				.output(TeamSummaryComparisonSchema),
			successRate: oc.input(DaysInputSchema).output(SuccessRateSchema),
		},
		developers: {
			list: oc.input(DaysInputSchema).output(z.array(DeveloperSummarySchema)),
			details: oc
				.input(DeveloperDetailsInputSchema)
				.output(DeveloperDetailsSchema),
			sessions: oc
				.input(DeveloperSessionsInputSchema)
				.output(z.array(DeveloperSessionSchema)),
			projects: oc
				.input(DeveloperDetailsInputSchema)
				.output(z.array(DeveloperProjectSchema)),
			timeline: oc
				.input(DeveloperDetailsInputSchema)
				.output(z.array(DeveloperTimelineSchema)),
			features: oc
				.input(DeveloperDetailsInputSchema)
				.output(DeveloperFeatureUsageSchema),
			errors: oc
				.input(DeveloperDetailsInputSchema)
				.output(z.array(DeveloperErrorSchema)),
			trends: oc
				.input(DaysInputSchema)
				.output(z.array(DeveloperTrendDataPointSchema)),
		},
		projects: {
			investment: oc
				.input(DaysInputSchema)
				.output(z.array(ProjectInvestmentSchema)),
			trends: oc
				.input(DaysInputSchema)
				.output(z.array(ProjectTrendDataPointSchema)),
			details: oc
				.input(ProjectDetailsInputSchema)
				.output(ProjectDetailDataSchema),
			contributors: oc
				.input(ProjectDetailsInputSchema)
				.output(z.array(ProjectContributorSchema)),
			features: oc
				.input(ProjectDetailsInputSchema)
				.output(ProjectFeatureUsageSchema),
			errors: oc
				.input(ProjectDetailsInputSchema)
				.output(z.array(ProjectErrorSchema)),
		},
		sessions: {
			list: oc
				.input(SessionListInputSchema)
				.output(z.array(SessionAnalyticsSchema)),
			summary: oc.input(DaysInputSchema).output(SessionAnalyticsSummarySchema),
			summaryComparison: oc
				.input(DaysInputSchema)
				.output(SessionAnalyticsSummaryComparisonSchema),
			dimensionAnalysis: oc
				.input(DimensionAnalysisInputSchema)
				.output(z.array(DimensionAnalysisDataPointSchema)),
			detail: oc.input(SessionDetailInputSchema).output(SessionDetailSchema),
		},
		roi: {
			metrics: oc.input(DaysInputSchema).output(ROIMetricsSchema),
			trends: oc.input(DaysInputSchema).output(z.array(ROITrendSchema)),
			breakdownDevelopers: oc
				.input(DaysInputSchema)
				.output(z.array(DeveloperCostBreakdownSchema)),
			breakdownProjects: oc
				.input(DaysInputSchema)
				.output(z.array(ProjectCostBreakdownSchema)),
		},
		errors: {
			topRecurring: oc
				.input(RecurringErrorsInputSchema)
				.output(z.array(RecurringErrorSchema)),
			trends: oc
				.input(ErrorTrendsInputSchema)
				.output(z.array(ErrorTrendDataPointSchema)),
		},
		learnings: {
			list: oc
				.input(PaginatedDaysInputSchema)
				.output(z.array(LearningEntrySchema)),
			stats: oc.input(DaysInputSchema).output(LearningsFeedStatsSchema),
			users: oc.output(z.array(z.string())),
			projects: oc.output(z.array(z.string())),
			trend: oc
				.input(LearningsTrendInputSchema)
				.output(z.array(LearningsTrendDataPointSchema)),
		},
		users: {
			mappings: oc.input(DaysInputSchema).output(z.array(UserMappingSchema)),
		},
	},
};

content.text:
All done. Here's a summary of what changed:

**New file:**
- `apps/web/src/hooks/useUserMap.ts` — derives `Record<string, string>` (userId → name) from `useFullOrganization`, cached by React Query

**Updated 8 frontend consumers** — replaced ~10 lines of ClickHouse fetch + useMemo boilerplate with `const { userMap } = useUserMap()`:
- `DevelopersListPage`, `DeveloperDetailPage`, `SessionsListPage`, `ProjectDetailPage`, `ROIPage`, `LearningsPage`, `ErrorsPage`, `Breadcrumb`

**Updated 4 chart components** — `Map<string, string>` → `Record<string, string>`:
- `DeveloperTrendChart`, `DimensionAnalysisChart`, `LearningsTrendChart`, `ErrorTrendChart`

**Updated `overview.service.ts`** — replaced `getUserMapping` (ClickHouse regex) with a direct Postgres `user` table lookup for the "top contributor" insight

**Deleted:**
- `apps/api/src/services/user.service.ts`
- `apps/api/src/handlers/analytics/users.ts`
- `analytics.users.mappings` contract + `UserMappingSchema` from `api-routes`

I need to understand the full picture of how user mappings are consumed across the frontend to plan a refactor. I already know the high-level pattern but need exact details.

Thoroughly explore:

1. **All pages that fetch `orpc.analytics.users.mappings`** - Read the relevant sections of each file to understand:
   - How they build the userMap (Map vs Record)
   - How they pass it down to child components
   - Whether they use `useAnalyticsQuery` wrapper or direct query

   Files to check:
   - apps/web/src/pages/dashboard/DevelopersListPage.tsx
   - apps/web/src/pages/dashboard/DeveloperDetailPage.tsx
   - apps/web/src/pages/dashboard/SessionsListPage.tsx
   - apps/web/src/pages/dashboard/ProjectDetailPage.tsx
   - apps/web/src/pages/dashboard/ROIPage.tsx
   - apps/web/src/pages/dashboard/LearningsPage.tsx
   - apps/web/src/pages/dashboard/ErrorsPage.tsx
   - apps/web/src/components/analytics/Breadcrumb.tsx

2. **The `useAnalyticsQuery` hook** - Read apps/web/src/hooks/useAnalyticsQuery.ts to understand how it wraps React Query

3. **Child components that receive userMap** - Check the prop types of:
   - apps/web/src/components/charts/DeveloperTrendChart.tsx
   - apps/web/src/components/charts/DimensionAnalysisChart.tsx
   - apps/web/src/components/charts/LearningsTrendChart.tsx
   - apps/web/src/components/charts/ErrorTrendChart.tsx

   - apps/web/src/components/learnings/TimelineItem.tsx

4. **The `useFullOrganization` hook** - already know it returns members with userId and user.name. Check if there's any caching/dedup behavior in React Query for this.

For each file, note:
- Whether userMap is a `Map<string, string>` or `Record<string, string>`
- The exact variable names used
- What props are passed to children

Be very thorough - read the actual code sections.

data.prompt:
I need to understand the full picture of how user mappings are consumed across the frontend to plan a refactor. I already know the high-level pattern but need exact details.

Thoroughly explore:

1. **All pages that fetch `orpc.analytics.users.mappings`** - Read the relevant sections of each file to understand:
   - How they build the userMap (Map vs Record)
   - How they pass it down to child components
   - Whether they use `useAnalyticsQuery` wrapper or direct query

   Files to check:
   - apps/web/src/pages/dashboard/DevelopersListPage.tsx
   - apps/web/src/pages/dashboard/DeveloperDetailPage.tsx
   - apps/web/src/pages/dashboard/SessionsListPage.tsx
   - apps/web/src/pages/dashboard/ProjectDetailPage.tsx
   - apps/web/src/pages/dashboard/ROIPage.tsx
   - apps/web/src/pages/dashboard/LearningsPage.tsx
   - apps/web/src/pages/dashboard/ErrorsPage.tsx
   - apps/web/src/components/analytics/Breadcrumb.tsx

2. **The `useAnalyticsQuery` hook** - Read apps/web/src/hooks/useAnalyticsQuery.ts to understand how it wraps React Query

3. **Child components that receive userMap** - Check the prop types of:
   - apps/web/src/components/charts/DeveloperTrendChart.tsx
   - apps/web/src/components/charts/DimensionAnalysisChart.tsx
   - apps/web/src/components/charts/LearningsTrendChart.tsx
   - apps/web/src/components/charts/ErrorTrendChart.tsx
   - apps/web/src/components/learnings/LearningsTimeline.tsx
   - apps/web/src/components/learnings/TimelineItem.tsx

4. **The `useFullOrganization` hook** - already know it returns members with userId and user.name. Check if there's any caching/dedup behavior in React Query for this.

5. **The API contract** - Check if `analytics.users.mappings` is used anywhere else (backend tests, other imports).

For each file, note:
- Whether userMap is a `Map<string, string>` or `Record<string, string>`
- The exact variable names used
- What props are passed to children

Be very thorough - read the actual code sections.

1→import { z } from "zod";
     2→import { SourceSchema } from "./source.js";
     3→
     4→// ── Common inputs ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
     5→
     6→export const DaysInputSchema = z.object({
     7→	days: z.number().int().positive().default(7),
     8→});
     9→
    10→export const PaginatedDaysInputSchema = DaysInputSchema.extend({
    11→	limit: z.number().int().positive().default(100),
    12→	offset: z.number().int().nonnegative().default(0),
    13→});
    14→
    15→export const DateRangeInputSchema = z.object({
    16→	startDate: z.string().date(),
    17→	endDate: z.string().date(),
    18→});
    19→
    20→// ── Overview ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    21→
    22→export const OverviewKPIsSchema = z.object({
    23→	distinct_users: z.number(),
    24→	distinct_sessions: z.number(),
    25→	distinct_projects: z.number(),
    26→	distinct_subagents: z.number(),
    27→	distinct_skills: z.number(),
    28→	distinct_slash_commands: z.number(),
    29→});
    30→
    31→export const UsageTrendDataSchema = z.object({
    32→	date: z.string(),
    33→	sessions: z.number(),
    34→	active_users: z.number(),
    35→	total_hours: z.number(),
    36→	total_tokens: z.number(),
    37→});
    38→
    39→export const ModelTokensTrendDataSchema = z.object({
    40→	date: z.string(),
    41→	model: z.string(),
    42→	total_tokens: z.number(),
    43→	input_tokens: z.number(),
    44→	output_tokens: z.number(),
    45→});
    46→
    47→export const InsightSchema = z.object({
    48→	type: z.enum(["trend", "performer", "alert", "info"]),
    49→	severity: z.enum(["positive", "warning", "negative", "info"]),
    50→	message: z.string(),
    51→	link: z.string().optional(),
    52→});
    53→
    54→export const TeamSummaryComparisonSchema = z.object({
    55→	current: z.object({
    56→		total_sessions: z.number(),
    57→		active_users: z.number(),
    58→		avg_duration_min: z.number(),
    59→		avg_sessions_per_user: z.number(),
    60→	}),
    61→	previous: z.object({
    62→		total_sessions: z.number(),
    63→		active_users: z.number(),
    64→		avg_duration_min: z.number(),
    65→		avg_sessions_per_user: z.number(),
    66→	}),
    67→	changes: z.object({
    68→		total_sessions: z.number(),
    69→		active_users: z.number(),
    70→		avg_duration_min: z.number(),
    71→		avg_sessions_per_user: z.number(),
    72→	}),
    73→});
    74→
    75→export const SuccessRateSchema = z.object({
    76→	current: z.object({
    77→		high_quality_sessions: z.number(),
    78→		total_sessions: z.number(),
    79→		success_rate: z.number(),
    80→	}),
    81→	previous: z.object({
    82→		high_quality_sessions: z.number(),
    83→		total_sessions: z.number(),
    84→		success_rate: z.number(),
    85→	}),
    86→	changes: z.object({
    87→		success_rate: z.number(),
    88→	}),
    89→});
    90→
    91→// ── Developers ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    92→
    93→export const DeveloperSummarySchema = z.object({
    94→	user_id: z.string(),
    95→	total_sessions: z.number(),
    96→	active_days: z.number(),
    97→	total_tokens: z.number(),
    98→	input_tokens: z.number(),
    99→	output_tokens: z.number(),
   100→	total_duration_min: z.number(),
   101→	avg_session_duration_min: z.number(),
   102→	last_active_date: z.string(),
   103→	success_rate: z.number(),
   104→	cost: z.number(),
   105→	success_rate_trend: z.number(),
   106→});
   107→
   108→export const DeveloperDetailsSchema = DeveloperSummarySchema.extend({
   109→	distinct_projects: z.number(),
   110→	error_count: z.number(),
   111→});
   112→
   113→export const DeveloperSessionSchema = z.object({
   114→	session_id: z.string(),
   115→	session_date: z.string(),
   116→	project_path: z.string(),
   117→	duration_min: z.number(),
   118→	total_tokens: z.number(),
   119→	has_subagents: z.boolean(),
   120→	has_skills: z.boolean(),
   121→	has_slash_commands: z.boolean(),
   122→	has_errors: z.boolean(),
   123→	likely_success: z.boolean(),
   124→});
   125→
   126→export const DeveloperProjectSchema = z.object({
   127→	project_path: z.string(),
   128→	sessions: z.number(),
   129→	total_duration_min: z.number(),
   130→	total_tokens: z.number(),
   131→	first_session: z.string(),
   132→	last_session: z.string(),
   133→});
   134→
   135→export const DeveloperTimelineSchema = z.object({
   136→	date: z.string(),
   137→	sessions: z.number(),
   138→	total_duration_min: z.number(),
   139→	total_tokens: z.number(),
   140→});
   141→
   142→export const DeveloperTrendDataPointSchema = z.object({
   143→	date: z.string(),
   144→	user_id: z.string(),
   145→	sessions: z.number(),
   146→	total_hours: z.number(),
   147→	total_tokens: z.number(),
   148→	avg_success_rate: z.number(),
   149→});
   150→

in the sessions page,  Recent Sessions Table filter for developers, it still shows an ID, not the usres.

content.text: Now let me see the MultiSelect usage for users:

message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Analysis:
Let me chronologically analyze the conversation:

1. **Initial request**: User asked how developer names are resolved in the developer screen, suspecting issues with migrated sessions from an old app with different user IDs.

2. **Research phase**: I explored the codebase and found that developer names were resolved via `user.service.ts` which extracted OS usernames from project paths using regex `/^\/(?:Users|home)\/([^/]+)\//` in ClickHouse queries. This was fragile and wouldn't work for migrated sessions.

3. **User asked about user_id ingestion**: Confirmed `user_id` is in the ClickHouse schema (`base-sessions.ts` line 24) and is set to `context.user.id` (Postgres auth user ID) during ingestion.

4. **User's key decision**: Instead of looking up distinct user_ids from ClickHouse then matching to Postgres, the user said to simply fetch all org members from Postgres in the frontend since orgs are small. They said: "we can just in the frontend somewhere fetch all users for the current org, and then use that for the mapping everywhere in the frontend."

5. **Plan approved**: Create `useUserMap` hook using `useFullOrganization`, replace all 8 call sites, update chart components from Map→Record, delete backend ClickHouse name resolution.

6. **Implementation completed**:
   - Created `useUserMap.ts` hook
   - Updated 8 consumer files (DevelopersListPage, DeveloperDetailPage, SessionsListPage, ProjectDetailPage, ROIPage, LearningsPage, ErrorsPage, Breadcrumb)
   - Updated 4 chart components (DeveloperTrendChart, DimensionAnalysisChart, LearningsTrendChart, ErrorTrendChart)
   - Updated `overview.service.ts` to use Postgres lookup instead of ClickHouse regex
   - Deleted `user.service.ts`, `users.ts` handler, removed `UserMappingSchema` from api-routes
   - Removed `analytics.users` route from analytics index

7. **Verification**: `bun run verify` passed after fixing one unused `useMemo` import in ErrorsPage.

8. **Latest user feedback**: User reported two issues with the Sessions page:
   - The "Recent Sessions" table filter for developers still shows user IDs, not names
   - The filter dropdown options are derived from `user_id`s in the session response, but not all users may appear in recent sessions. User wants filter values loaded from org members (same source as `useUserMap`), not from session data.

9. **Current state of SessionsListPage**: 
   - `users` memo (line 219-224) derives filter options from `sessions.map(s => s.user_id)` - raw IDs from ClickHouse
   - `MultiSelect` at line 434-440 passes these raw IDs as `options` and `selected`
   - No name mapping is applied to the filter dropdown options
   - `selectedUsers` state stores raw user IDs and filters against `session.user_id`

The fix needs to:
1. Replace the `users` memo with org member data from `userMap`
2. Show display names in the MultiSelect dropdown
3. Map between display names and user IDs for filtering

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user initially asked how developer names are resolved in the developer screen, suspecting issues with migrated sessions having different user IDs. This led to a refactor to replace the ClickHouse regex-based name resolution (extracting OS username from project paths) with Postgres org member lookups. The user explicitly requested: "we need to get 100% rid of the clickhouse regex. all developers and users need to be resolved from the postgres database." The user's latest request is to fix the Sessions page developer filter dropdown which still shows raw user IDs instead of names, and to source the filter options from org members rather than from session response data.

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Monorepo: Bun + Turborepo + TypeScript (apps/api, apps/web, apps/cli, packages/*)
   - Frontend: React SPA with Vite, TanStack React Query, @orpc for RPC
   - Auth: better-auth with organizations, members linked via Postgres
   - Data: ClickHouse for session analytics, Postgres (Neon) for auth/org data
   - `useFullOrganization` hook fetches org members with `userId` and `user.name` from Postgres via better-auth
   - `useUserMap` hook (new) derives `Record<string, string>` (userId → name) from `useFullOrganization`
   - `formatUsername(userId, userMap)` utility for display with fallback to raw ID
   - `MultiSelect` component for filter dropdowns

3. Files and Code Sections:

   - **`apps/web/src/hooks/useUserMap.ts`** (NEW - core of the refactor)
     ```typescript
     import { useMemo } from "react";
     import { useOrganization } from "@/contexts/OrganizationContext";
     import { useFullOrganization } from "@/hooks/useFullOrganization";

     export function useUserMap() {
       const { activeOrg } = useOrganization();
       const { data: fullOrg, isLoading } = useFullOrganization(activeOrg?.id);

       const userMap = useMemo(() => {
         const record: Record<string, string> = {};
         if (fullOrg?.members) {
           for (const m of fullOrg.members) {
             record[m.userId] = m.user.name;
           }
         }
         return record;
       }, [fullOrg]);

       return { userMap, isLoading };
     }
     ```

   - **`apps/web/src/hooks/useFullOrganization.ts`** (existing, provides member data)
     - Returns `FullOrg` with `members[]` containing `{ userId, user: { id, name, email, image } }`
     - Cached by React Query via `["full-organization", orgId]` key

   - **`apps/web/src/pages/dashboard/SessionsListPage.tsx`** (CURRENT FOCUS - needs more fixes)
     - Replaced ClickHouse `userMappings` fetch with `useUserMap()` hook
     - But the `users` filter options (lines 219-224) still derive from session data:
       ```typescript
       const users = useMemo(() => {
         if (!sessions) return [];
         return Array.from(
           new Set(sessions.map((s) => s.user_id).filter(Boolean)),
         ).sort();
       }, [sessions]);
       ```
     - MultiSelect passes raw user IDs (lines 434-440):
       ```typescript
       <MultiSelect
         options={users}
         selected={selectedUsers}
         onChange={setSelectedUsers}
         placeholder="All Developers"
         className="w-48"
       />
       ```
     - `selectedUsers` state stores raw user IDs, filtering matches `session.user_id`

   - **`apps/web/src/pages/dashboard/DevelopersListPage.tsx`** - Updated: removed `userMappings` fetch, uses `useUserMap()`, replaced `userMapRecord` with `userMap`
   - **`apps/web/src/pages/dashboard/DeveloperDetailPage.tsx`** - Updated: same pattern
   - **`apps/web/src/pages/dashboard/ProjectDetailPage.tsx`** - Updated: same pattern
   - **`apps/web/src/pages/dashboard/ROIPage.tsx`** - Updated: same pattern
   - **`apps/web/src/pages/dashboard/LearningsPage.tsx`** - Updated: replaced `Map` usage with `Record`, `userMap.get()` → `userMap[]`, removed `Object.fromEntries(userMap)` conversions
   - **`apps/web/src/pages/dashboard/ErrorsPage.tsx`** - Updated: removed `useMemo` import (was unused after refactor)
   - **`apps/web/src/components/analytics/Breadcrumb.tsx`** - Updated: removed `useAnalyticsQuery`/`orpc`/`useMemo` imports, simplified to `useUserMap()`

   - **Chart components** (all updated `Map<string, string>` → `Record<string, string>`):
     - `DeveloperTrendChart.tsx` - `userMap.get(userId)` → `userMap[userId]`
     - `DimensionAnalysisChart.tsx` - removed `Object.fromEntries(userMap || new Map())` conversion
     - `LearningsTrendChart.tsx` - `userMap.get(key)` → `userMap[key]`
     - `ErrorTrendChart.tsx` - `userMap.get(key)` → `userMap[key]`

   - **DELETED files:**
     - `apps/api/src/services/user.service.ts` - contained `extractUsernameFromPath`, `getUserMappings`, `getUserMapping`
     - `apps/api/src/handlers/analytics/users.ts` - contained `analytics.users.mappings` handler

   - **`apps/api/src/handlers/analytics/index.ts`** - Removed `usersRouter` import and route
   - **`apps/api/src/services/overview.service.ts`** - Replaced `getUserMapping` (ClickHouse regex) with direct Postgres user lookup:
     ```typescript
     const [userData] = await db
       .select({ name: user.name })
       .from(user)
       .where(eq(user.id, performer.user_id))
       .limit(1);
     const displayName = userData?.name || `${performer.user_id.substring(0, 8)}...`;
     ```
   - **`packages/api-routes/src/index.ts`** - Removed `UserMappingSchema` import and `analytics.users` contract
   - **`packages/api-routes/src/schemas/analytics.ts`** - Removed `UserMappingSchema` and `UserMapping` type

   - **`apps/web/src/lib/format.ts`** (unchanged, still used):
     ```typescript
     export function formatUsername(userId: string, userMap?: Record<string, string>): string {
       if (userMap?.[userId]) { return userMap[userId]; }
       return userId;
     }
     ```

4. Errors and fixes:
   - **Unused `useMemo` import in ErrorsPage.tsx**: After removing the ClickHouse mapping boilerplate, `useMemo` was no longer used. TypeScript and Biome both flagged it. Fixed by changing `import { useMemo, useState }` to `import { useState }`.
   - **Linter race condition on LearningsPage.tsx**: While editing `Object.fromEntries(userMap)` → `userMap`, the linter modified the file between reads. Had to re-read the file and re-apply the edit.
   - **"File has not been read yet" errors**: Several Edit tool calls failed because the tool requires a fresh Read before editing. Had to re-read files before making edits.

5. Problem Solving:
   - Identified that name resolution was fragile (regex on project paths) and broken for migrated sessions
   - User rejected the approach of querying distinct user_ids from ClickHouse then looking up in Postgres - too complex, just fetch all org members
   - Chose `Record<string, string>` as the canonical type (instead of `Map`) since it matches `formatUsername` signature and is simpler
   - Replaced the `getUserMapping` call in `overview.service.ts` with a direct Postgres lookup (was a dynamic import that would have broken after deleting `user.service.ts`)

6. All user messages:
   - "how are currently the names of developers in the developer screen resolved? I am asking because I see some of them not being resolved, but it could be due to us migrating sessions from the old app, where user ids were different."
   - "okay are we ingesting the user_id on the sessions?"
   - "Continue from where you left off."
   - "is it in the schem (check in @packages/ch-schema/src/db/schema/base-sessions.ts if so we want to use the session ids, and then replace the user_ids by the usernames of the members of the organization)"
   - "i think we can scipt the distinct user_id lookup. we can just in the frontend somewhere fetch all users for the current org, and then use that for the mapping everywhere in the frontend. since the size of orgs is not too large, we dont need add the extra step of fetching distcint user ids..... we will always be in the context of an org"
   - "yes we need to get 100% rid of the clickhouse regex. all developers and users need to be resolved from the postgres database. Implement the usermapping in the most efficient way, either by reusing the useFullOrganiation or some other way. you decide"
   - "in the sessions page, Recent Sessions Table filter for developers, it still shows an ID, not the usres. Also the filter dropdown should not be derived from the user_ids in the response. since e..g if not all users are in the recent sessions, we cant filter. so load the filter values how you load the user mapping. and then allow filtering the 'recent' sessions by those values."

7. Pending Tasks:
   - Fix the Sessions page developer filter to show names instead of IDs
   - Source filter options from org members (via `useUserMap`) instead of from session response data
   - Maintain filtering functionality (map display names back to user IDs for actual filtering)

8. Current Work:
   I had just read the relevant sections of `SessionsListPage.tsx` to understand the current filter implementation when the user pointed out two issues. The `users` memo at lines 219-224 derives filter options from session data (`sessions.map(s => s.user_id)`), and the `MultiSelect` at lines 434-440 displays these raw user IDs. The fix needs to:
   1. Replace the `users` memo with org member data from `userMap` (show all org members as filter options, not just those in current sessions)
   2. Display member names in the dropdown instead of raw IDs
   3. Map between display names and user IDs for the actual session filtering logic

9. Optional Next Step:
   Fix the `SessionsListPage.tsx` developer filter as the user explicitly requested: "the filter dropdown should not be derived from the user_ids in the response. since e.g if not all users are in the recent sessions, we cant filter. so load the filter values how you load the user mapping."

   Specifically:
   - Replace the `users` memo with entries from `userMap` (Object.keys/entries)
   - Show display names in MultiSelect options
   - Track `selectedUsers` as user IDs internally but display names to the user
   - Filter sessions by matching `session.user_id` against selected user IDs

# Plan: Replace ClickHouse regex name resolution with Postgres org members

## Context

Developer names are currently resolved by extracting OS usernames from `project_path` in ClickHouse (`/Users/marc/...` → `marc`). This is fragile and breaks for migrated sessions with different `organization_id`s. Since every user is already a member of a Postgres organization with a real `name`, we should resolve names from there instead.

## Approach

Create a `useUserMap` hook that derives user mappings from `useFullOrganization`, then replace all 8 call sites. Remove the ClickHouse-based `analytics.users.mappings` endpoint entirely.

## Changes

### 1. Create `apps/web/src/hooks/useUserMap.ts`

New hook that wraps `useFullOrganization`:
- Gets `activeOrg` from `useOrganization()` context
- Calls `useFullOrganization(activeOrg?.id)` (already cached by React Query via `["full-organization", orgId]` key)
- Returns `{ userMap: Record<string, string>, isLoading: boolean }` where keys are `member.userId` → `member.user.name`
- Single canonical type (`Record<string, string>`) — child components expecting `Map` will be updated

### 2. Update 8 consumer files (remove `userMappings` fetch + `useMemo` boilerplate)

Each file currently has ~10 lines of boilerplate to fetch + transform. Replace with `const { userMap } = useUserMap()`.

| File | Current pattern | Notes |
|------|----------------|-------|
| `DevelopersListPage.tsx` | Map + Record | Already imports `useFullOrganization` — remove that too, `useUserMap` handles it |
| `DeveloperDetailPage.tsx` | Record | |
| `SessionsListPage.tsx` | Map + Record | |
| `ProjectDetailPage.tsx` | Record | |
| `ROIPage.tsx` | Record | |
| `LearningsPage.tsx` | Map | Uses Map for filter display + passes Record to timeline |
| `ErrorsPage.tsx` | Map | |
| `Breadcrumb.tsx` | Record | Has `enabled` flag — `useUserMap` always fetches (fine, React Query caches) |

### 3. Update child components: `Map<string, string>` → `Record<string, string>`

Components that currently accept `Map<string, string>` will switch to `Record<string, string>` (simpler, matches `formatUsername` signature):

| Component | Prop change |
|-----------|------------|
| `DeveloperTrendChart.tsx` | `userMap: Map<string, string>` → `Record<string, string>` |
| `DimensionAnalysisChart.tsx` | `userMap?: Map<string, string>` → `Record<string, string>` |
| `LearningsTrendChart.tsx` | `userMap?: Map<string, string>` → `Record<string, string>` |
| `ErrorTrendChart.tsx` | `userMap?: Map<string, string>` → `Record<string, string>` |

`LearningsTimeline.tsx` and `TimelineItem.tsx` already use `Record` — no change needed.

Internal usage changes: `userMap.get(key)` → `userMap[key]`, remove `Object.fromEntries()` conversions.

### 4. Delete backend ClickHouse name resolution

- **Delete** `apps/api/src/services/user.service.ts` (entire file — `extractUsernameFromPath`, `getUserMappings`, `getUserMapping`)
- **Remove** `analytics.users.mappings` handler from `apps/api/src/handlers/analytics/users.ts`
- **Remove** `analytics.users` route from `apps/api/src/handlers/analytics/index.ts`
- **Remove** `UserMappingSchema` from `packages/api-routes/src/schemas/analytics.ts` and its export/contract entry in `packages/api-routes/src/index.ts`

### 5. Cleanup

- Remove `formatUsername` import and `userMapRecord`/`userMap` variable declarations from all updated pages
- Remove unused imports (`useAnalyticsQuery` if no longer needed in a file, etc.)

## Verification

1. `bun run verify` — type checking, linting, tests pass
2. Manual: run `bun run dev:local`, navigate to Developers, Sessions, Projects, ROI, Learnings, Errors pages — developer names should display correctly

# Plan: Replace ClickHouse regex name resolution with Postgres org members

## Context

Developer names are currently resolved by extracting OS usernames from `project_path` in ClickHouse (`/Users/marc/...` → `marc`). This is fragile and breaks for migrated sessions with different `organization_id`s. Since every user is already a member of a Postgres organization with a real `name`, we should resolve names from there instead.

## Approach

Create a `useUserMap` hook that derives user mappings from `useFullOrganization`, then replace all 8 call sites. Remove the ClickHouse-based `analytics.users.mappings` endpoint entirely.

## Changes

### 1. Create `apps/web/src/hooks/useUserMap.ts`

New hook that wraps `useFullOrganization`:
- Gets `activeOrg` from `useOrganization()` context
- Calls `useFullOrganization(activeOrg?.id)` (already cached by React Query via `["full-organization", orgId]` key)
- Returns `{ userMap: Record<string, string>, isLoading: boolean }` where keys are `member.userId` → `member.user.name`
- Single canonical type (`Record<string, string>`) — child components expecting `Map` will be updated

### 2. Update 8 consumer files (remove `userMappings` fetch + `useMemo` boilerplate)

Each file currently has ~10 lines of boilerplate to fetch + transform. Replace with `const { userMap } = useUserMap()`.

| File | Current pattern | Notes |
|------|----------------|-------|
| `DevelopersListPage.tsx` | Map + Record | Already imports `useFullOrganization` — remove that too, `useUserMap` handles it |
| `DeveloperDetailPage.tsx` | Record | |
| `SessionsListPage.tsx` | Map + Record | |
| `ProjectDetailPage.tsx` | Record | |
| `ROIPage.tsx` | Record | |
| `LearningsPage.tsx` | Map | Uses Map for filter display + passes Record to timeline |
| `ErrorsPage.tsx` | Map | |
| `Breadcrumb.tsx` | Record | Has `enabled` flag — `useUserMap` always fetches (fine, React Query caches) |

### 3. Update child components: `Map<string, string>` → `Record<string, string>`

Components that currently accept `Map<string, string>` will switch to `Record<string, string>` (simpler, matches `formatUsername` signature):

| Component | Prop change |
|-----------|------------|
| `DeveloperTrendChart.tsx` | `userMap: Map<string, string>` → `Record<string, string>` |
| `DimensionAnalysisChart.tsx` | `userMap?: Map<string, string>` → `Record<string, string>` |
| `LearningsTrendChart.tsx` | `userMap?: Map<string, string>` → `Record<string, string>` |
| `ErrorTrendChart.tsx` | `userMap?: Map<string, string>` → `Record<string, string>` |

`LearningsTimeline.tsx` and `TimelineItem.tsx` already use `Record` — no change needed.

Internal usage changes: `userMap.get(key)` → `userMap[key]`, remove `Object.fromEntries()` conversions.

### 4. Delete backend ClickHouse name resolution

- **Delete** `apps/api/src/services/user.service.ts` (entire file — `extractUsernameFromPath`, `getUserMappings`, `getUserMapping`)
- **Remove** `analytics.users.mappings` handler from `apps/api/src/handlers/analytics/users.ts`
- **Remove** `analytics.users` route from `apps/api/src/handlers/analytics/index.ts`
- **Remove** `UserMappingSchema` from `packages/api-routes/src/schemas/analytics.ts` and its export/contract entry in `packages/api-routes/src/index.ts`

### 5. Cleanup

- Remove `formatUsername` import and `userMapRecord`/`userMap` variable declarations from all updated pages
- Remove unused imports (`useAnalyticsQuery` if no longer needed in a file, etc.)

## Verification

1. `bun run verify` — type checking, linting, tests pass
2. Manual: run `bun run dev:local`, navigate to Developers, Sessions, Projects, ROI, Learnings, Errors pages — developer names should display correctly

lines: -# Plan: Replace ClickHouse regex name resolution with Postgres org members
+# Plan: Fix Sessions page developer filter (remaining work)

-Developer names are currently resolved by extracting OS usernames from `project_path` in ClickHouse (`/Users/marc/...` → `marc`). This is fragile and breaks for migrated sessions with different `organization_id`s. Since every user is already a member of a Postgres organization with a real `name`, we should resolve names from there instead.

+Steps 1–5 of the original plan (create `useUserMap`, update 8 consumers, update chart components, delete ClickHouse name resolution, cleanup) are **complete** and verified. The remaining issue: the Sessions page developer filter dropdown still shows raw user IDs and derives its options from session response data instead of org members.
-Create a `useUserMap` hook that derives user mappings from `useFullOrganization`, then replace all 8 call sites. Remove the ClickHouse-based `analytics.users.mappings` endpoint entirely.
+- The `users` memo (line 219-224) builds filter options from `sessions.map(s => s.user_id)` — raw IDs from ClickHouse
+- The `MultiSelect` (line 434-440) passes those raw IDs as `options` and `selected`
+- Users not present in recent sessions can't be filtered for
+### A. Replace `users` memo with org-member-derived options
-- Calls `useFullOrganization(activeOrg?.id)` (already cached by React Query via `["full-organization", orgId]` key)
-- Returns `{ userMap: Record<string, string>, isLoading: boolean }` where keys are `member.userId` → `member.user.name`
-- Single canonical type (`Record<string, string>`) — child components expecting `Map` will be updated
-Each file currently has ~10 lines of boilerplate to fetch + transform. Replace with `const { userMap } = useUserMap()`.
-| `DevelopersListPage.tsx` | Map + Record | Already imports `useFullOrganization` — remove that too, `useUserMap` handles it |
-| `LearningsPage.tsx` | Map | Uses Map for filter display + passes Record to timeline |
-| `Breadcrumb.tsx` | Record | Has `enabled` flag — `useUserMap` always fetches (fine, React Query caches) |
-Components that currently accept `Map<string, string>` will switch to `Record<string, string>` (simpler, matches `formatUsername` signature):
+This shows display names in the dropdown, sources all org members as options (not just those in recent sessions), and maps back to user IDs for filtering.
+No other files need changes — `filteredSessions` already filters by `selectedUsers` containing user IDs matched against `session.user_id`.
-`LearningsTimeline.tsx` and `TimelineItem.tsx` already use `Record` — no change needed.
-- **Remove** `UserMappingSchema` from `packages/api-routes/src/schemas/analytics.ts` and its export/contract entry in `packages/api-routes/src/index.ts`
-- Remove `formatUsername` import and `userMapRecord`/`userMap` variable declarations from all updated pages
-- Remove unused imports (`useAnalyticsQuery` if no longer needed in a file, etc.)
1. `bun run verify` — type checking, linting, tests pass
-2. Manual: run `bun run dev:local`, navigate to Developers, Sessions, Projects, ROI, Learnings, Errors pages — developer names should display correctly
+2. Manual: Sessions page filter dropdown shows member names, filtering works correctly

1. **Replace `users` memo** — derive filter options from `userMap` (all org members) instead of from session data
2. **Update MultiSelect** — show display names, map back to user IDs on selection
